license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.w8vfhw6
title: Adapted protocol to extract total RNA using TRIzol® (Invitrogen, Thermo)
author: Talita B Gagliardi

steps_raw.instruction: Incubate the homogeneized sample for 5min at r.t.
Shake the tube vigorously by vortex for 15sec.
Centrifuge for 15min at 12,000 x g and 4ºC.
The mixture separates into red lower phenol-chloroform phase, and interphase, and a colorless upper aqueous phase. RNA remains in the upper phase while the DNA and proteins in the interphase.
Place the aqueous phase for a new microfuge tube.RNA IsolationRNA precipitation
Centrifuge for 30min at 12,000 x g and 4ºC.
Centrifuge for 5min at 7,500 x g and 4ºC.
Ressuspend the RNA pellet in 30uL of RNAse-free H2O at 55-60ºC.
In a microfuge tube: 750ul of TRIzol (Invitrogen, Thermo)+ 250ul of sample
